meaning: n.<英>橡皮底帆布鞋[缘此鞋橡皮底线形似Plimsoll line]
examples: After becoming the youngest boss of a publicly listed German company when he took over Puma in 1993, he transformed the firm from a near-bankrupt plimsoll-maker into a trendy high-street brand.
Samuel Plimsoll was born in Bristol on 10 February 1824, but died in the town of Folkestone 74 years later.
The cruel loss of life and the impoverished widows and children left behind inspired Plimsoll to take up the sailors' plight.
He is best remembered for creating the Plimsoll Line, which appears on the hull of seafaring ships to show safe loading limits, preventing accidents.
